title: Chapter 45
content:

It wasn’t a scream. It was a moan—a long, broken, high-pitched wail of surrender echoing from the second floor.

"Leng Yue!" Gu Ling shouted, panic tightening her chest. "Hang on! I’m coming!"

She burst onto the landing and skidded into the Hall of Mirrors. The air here was freezing, a sharp contrast to the humid rot below, but the smell... the smell was unmistakable. It was the scent of musk, sweat, and female arousal.

"Yue, are you—"

Gu Ling froze. The fire in her hand flickered and died, extinguished by sheer shock.

Her legs were spread wide in a vulgar ’M’ shape. Her head was thrown back, eyes rolled into her skull, mouth hanging open in a silent scream of ecstasy.

And her hand.

Gu Ling watched in horror as Leng Yue’s hand moved with a blur of speed between her legs.

"What the fuck..." Gu Ling whispered, her stomach churning. "Yue! Snap out of it!"

Leng Yue didn’t even register her presence. Her body was slippery with sweat. She simply shrugged off Gu Ling’s hand and drove her fingers deeper, moaning louder. She was gone—lost in a labyrinth of her own corrupted mind.

Gu Ling scrambled back, stumbling to her feet. Her breathing was ragged. This wasn’t a fight. She could burn monsters, she could slash ghosts, but this? This was insanity.

"I need help," Gu Ling gasped. "I can’t... I need Teacher. I need Kai."

She spun around, intending to bolt back to the stairs.

She ran straight into a wall of solid, warm muscle.

"Going somewhere, brat?"

A heavy hand clamped down on her shoulder. The grip was iron-hard, the fingers digging into her trapezius with a familiarity that sent a jolt of lightning down her spine.

Gu Ling looked up, her heart hammering against her ribs.

"Kai?" she breathed. "How did you... were you already here?"

She stepped back, trying to put distance between them, but her eyes betrayed her. They traveled down.

He was standing in the shadows of the mirrors. He wasn’t wearing his uniform. He wasn’t wearing anything.

Gu Ling’s eyes widened to the size of saucers.

He was naked. Gloriously, terrifyingly naked. His body was a landscape of corded muscle and veins, glistening as if oiled. His chest was broad, tapering down to a waist that looked strong enough to break her.

"W-What the fuck are you doing, Kai?!" Gu Ling shrieked, her voice cracking. She tried to cover her eyes, but her fingers parted involuntarily. She couldn’t look away. "Get dressed! We are on a mission! Are you crazy?!"

The naked Kai didn’t move. He smirked—that same arrogant, infuriating smirk he had worn in the arena.

"Mission?" he scoffed, his voice a low, gravelly rumble that vibrated in her chest. "The only mission here is figuring out who needs to be disciplined."

He took a step toward her. The erection bobbed with the movement.

"Remember the arena, Gu Ling?"

Gu Ling flinched. "Shut up! That was... that was a duel!"

"Was it?" Kai purred. He loomed over her, backing her up until her back hit one of the cold mirrors. "I remember you lying across my lap. I remember pulling down those little pants. I remember the sound your ass made when I hit it."

"You acted so tough," Kai whispered, leaning down until his face was inches from hers. "But when my hand hit your ass... you liked it, didn’t you?"

"I didn’t!" Gu Ling yelled, her face burning hotter than her fire ever could. "I hated it! You humiliated me!"
